dialogue: A suburban home stands out among the abandoned neighborhood.
The windows are boarded. Smoke rises from a chimney.
Someone is still living here.

choices.text: Circle the house and look for another way in
choices.lockedText: You aren't confident enough to move quietly around the property.

dialogue: A slot in the door opens.
'State your business,' a tired voice says.

choices.lockedText: You struggle to make a convincing case.

dialogue: You slip around the property.
In the backyard, you find fresh graves.
Three of them.

choices.lockedText: You can't make out anything useful.

dialogue: The markers are recent.
One grave is smaller than the others.
You hear quiet crying from inside the house.
The homeowner eventually opens the door a few inches.
'We've lost enough people. We don't trust strangers.'
'But if you've seen a safe road north, I'll listen.'

choices.lockedText: You can't think of a convincing story.

dialogue: 'We've barely got enough for ourselves.'
The slot closes.
The conversation is over.
Several gun barrels appear through firing holes in the boards.
'Wrong house.'
The homeowner thanks you.
Before you leave, they hand you a small bag of supplies.
'Good luck out there.'
The homeowner believes you.
As you leave, guilt follows close behind.
If they head north, they may never come back.
You continue down the street.
Whatever story was inside that house remains unknown.
